caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is 4-digit 5415, while MSP and systems-integration employment spans adjacent NAICS codes.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Kaseya measures automation deployment, not labor hours eliminated by SOC role.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The residual includes unknown proportions of desk, field, infrastructure and administrative work.
inputs.pi_dist: The 2023 pricing survey predates the current service-desk AI cycle; client headcount and endpoint-base effects could offset rate retention.
inputs.pi_moat: MSP contracts may be cancellable and platforms can capture value through higher software fees; this is not a claim of proprietary technology.
inputs.t50_years: Global MSP survey rather than U.S. 541512 census; it does not establish full autonomous delivery.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Observed value is for a global MSP sample spanning adjacent IT-service classifications, not a NAICS 541512 sample.
inputs.historical_analogs: No code-specific historical labor or pricing series was located.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Advisory commentary is not a representative 541512 owner-demographic survey.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not infer the volume, geography or quality of available targets from this qualitative evidence.
inputs.active_consolidators: Examples are from 1H 2025 and do not enumerate all acquirers or prove interest in every 541512 subsegment.
inputs.buy_mult: The source's disclosed-deal median and size table are not a direct comp set for each screened target.
inputs.exit_mult: Aventis's higher transaction data are skewed to larger deals; Solganick reports premium valuations only for differentiated scalable platforms.
inputs.pricing_structure: The cited pricing mix is from the 2023 benchmark and applies to MSPs, a business-model frame broader than 541512.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

a.rationale: Task exposure is high across coding, support, QA, documentation, monitoring and administrative delivery. It remains partial for architecture, security accountability, field work and client-specific judgment. The 4-digit role mix has a large residual likely containing both additional technicians and physical/field roles, so it is not assumed fully automatable.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541512 ($168,217,056,007) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541512 ($278,318,454,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

sources.title: Kaseya, 2026 State of the MSP Report press release
sources.what_it_supports: Survey of more than 1,000 MSPs worldwide: 53% already use AI for ticketing, patching and monitoring; 48% rank AI/automation as the leading client need; cybersecurity and business-continuity revenue are growing. Used for operational adoption and terminal-demand context.

sources.title: Kaseya, MSP Pricing: A Guide to Managed IT Services Pricing
sources.what_it_supports: MSP pricing modes include per-user, per-device, combined, tiered and fixed-fee subscriptions; the source also explains that per-user revenue fluctuates with client employee counts. Its benchmark figures are from the 2023 survey, so pricing evidence is older than adoption evidence.

sources.title: Solganick & Co., Technology Services M&A Market Update, 1H 2025
sources.what_it_supports: MSP M&A activity rebounded in Q2 2025; private-equity interest remained robust for predictable/scalable revenue, and the report lists current platform acquisitions including an Evergreen Lyra MSP deal. Its advisory focus is firms with roughly $2M–$20M EBITDA, broader than every target in the screen.

sources.title: Aventis Advisors, MSP Valuation Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: Advisor analysis says smaller MSPs typically trade around 5–8x EBITDA, while larger MSPs receive higher multiples; its disclosed-transaction median is 8.9x but the median deal size is $38.5M. Used for conservative buy/exit assumptions and size-discount caveats.

sources.title: Excendio Advisors, Navigating Growth, Exit & Legacy as an MSP Owner
sources.what_it_supports: MSP-focused exit adviser describes a large cohort of entrepreneurial founders from the 1990s and early 2000s, many now in their 50s or older, and discusses consolidation and transition challenges. It is advisory commentary, not a representative owner-age census.

records.title: Computer Systems Design Services

caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is 5-digit proxy and residual is broad.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Certification is evidence about refrigerant tasks, not every plumbing task.
inputs.pi_dist: Residential versus commercial revenue mix varies materially.
inputs.pi_moat: Licensing supports demand but is not itself a pricing moat.
inputs.t50_years: Vendor-sponsored survey; AI agent use is not the same as effective firm-wide automation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Commercial MEP survey rather than exact NAICS census.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Not an observed NAICS owner-age statistic.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: This reduces confidence in buyability.
inputs.active_consolidators: Not a census of 238220 and consolidators are used only for B/M context.
inputs.buy_mult: BizBuySell reports SDE/owner earnings, not normalized EBITDA; larger targets can price higher.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct small-platform HVAC EBITDA exit series was reopened.
inputs.pricing_structure: Exact price-book share is not sourced.

sources.title: Section 608 Technician Certification | US EPA
sources.what_it_supports: EPA says covered refrigeration and air-conditioning technicians must be certified and pass an EPA-approved test.

sources.title: ServiceTitan Commercial Sales and Marketing Report
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 survey of over 1,000 primarily mechanical/electrical/plumbing commercial leaders: 29% used AI agents; current uses include content, lead qualification and CRM updates.

sources.title: HVAC Business Valuation Multiples & Financial Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Reported 2021–25 sold HVAC businesses averaged 2.75x owner earnings; sold-business earnings multiple quartiles were 1.99x, 2.58x, 2.75x and 3.33x.

sources.title: Capstone Partners Construction Services M&A Coverage Report, August 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Public construction-services index showed 18.9x average LTM EBITDA; it is a public-company comparator, not small HVAC deal evidence.

records.title: Plumbing, Heating, and Air-Conditioning Contractors

caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The sources survey developers or development organizations, not occupations within US NAICS 541511.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied role mix is four-digit 5415 and understates the code-delivery concentration of a pure custom-programming shop.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: This input measures technical task replaceability only; it does not take credit for adoption speed, client demand or pricing.
inputs.pi_dist: ISG covers broader application-development outsourcing and Globant is a scaled provider, not a US lower-middle-market 541511 sample.
inputs.pi_dist: No fresh source measures the T&M, staff-augmentation or fixed-price mix for the target EBITDA band.
inputs.pi_moat: The source includes managed services and global systems integrators, which often have stickier contracts than a project-oriented 541511 firm.
inputs.pi_moat: This is a capture input only and does not use human accountability to claim a terminal-demand floor.
inputs.t50_years: Tool use and self-reported productivity are not identical to effective adoption across all billable tasks.
inputs.t50_years: The evidence is not a small-firm, NAICS-specific longitudinal adoption measure.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: GitHub's survey was 500 US developers at companies with 1,000+ employees and predates the current tool generation.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Stack Overflow and DORA do not isolate US lower-middle-market custom-programming firms.
inputs.historical_analogs: This is qualitative context only and is not used to set adoption timing beyond the separate adoption inputs.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: This is not a measured owner-age rate and should not be read as evidence of a succession wave.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of a located source is not evidence that no individual firm faces succession risk.
inputs.active_consolidators: The evidence covers broad IT services, including MSPs, cloud and systems integration rather than a deal tally for 541511.
inputs.active_consolidators: This factor is used only for buyability and does not support adoption, capture or terminal demand.
inputs.buy_mult: This is a UK IT-services benchmark, not disclosed US lower-middle-market 541511 transactions.
inputs.buy_mult: Contractor pass-through and owner compensation normalization can materially change an apparent EBITDA multiple.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct evidence supports multiple expansion for a generic 541511 firm; the selected exit is conditional on contract and revenue-quality improvement.
inputs.exit_mult: The source mix includes managed services and cloud assets with more recurring revenue than custom programming.
inputs.pricing_structure: No source quantifies the current pricing-model mix for US $1-10M EBITDA 541511 operators.

caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The mandated SOC identities are a 4-digit 5419 proxy dominated by sibling industries, including veterinary services.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Freelance linguist cost is often outside W-2 wages, so labor-share V understates the cost base AI attacks.
inputs.pi_dist: Regulated interpreting and fixed/recurring contracts can retain more value than general per-word translation.
inputs.pi_moat: No company-specific contract, credential or customer-concentration data are provided.
inputs.t50_years: This is not a forecast of 50% replacement of live interpreters or all revenue lines.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No representative US NAICS 541930 adoption sample was found in reopened sources.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Do not interpret this as documented succession evidence.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of an observed source is not proof of no succession need.
inputs.active_consolidators: The 57 transactions are global and include varied language-industry segments, not a US small-LSP buyer count.
inputs.buy_mult: S5 is an adviser-published valuation range, not a transaction database; LanguageLine is a large historical strategic-platform comparator, not a small-target comp.
inputs.exit_mult: No company-specific revenue mix, recurring-contract evidence or recent closed small-LSP transaction is available.
inputs.pricing_structure: No code-wide US revenue or contract-pricing split is provided by reopened sources.

sources.title: Census NAICS 541930 definition
sources.what_it_supports: The exact NAICS scope includes written translation, speech interpretation and sign-language services, with specified exclusions for transcription and captioning.

sources.title: Preliminary Findings from the 2025 Nimdzi 100
sources.what_it_supports: Nimdzi says neural MT took four to six years to become mainstream, LLM adoption is repeating a gradual validation pattern, and traditional translation volume can increase while revenue declines.

sources.title: HHS Limited English Proficiency
sources.what_it_supports: HHS identifies interpreter/document translation needs and Title VI and Section 1557 language-access requirements for covered programs.

sources.title: Slator Research Hub
sources.what_it_supports: Slator's 2025 language-industry M&A and funding report says it analyzed 57 M&A transactions and 25 funding rounds.

sources.title: What's My Language Company Worth in 2025?
sources.what_it_supports: A language-services M&A adviser states that buyers use EBITDA multiples and reports 3-6x EBITDA for healthy LSPs, with 2-3.5x for firms under $3m revenue.

sources.title: Teleperformance agreement to acquire LanguageLine
sources.what_it_supports: The 2016 transaction announcement reports $1.522bn consideration, $388m revenue and $147m adjusted EBITDA for LanguageLine, a scaled language-access platform.

sources.title: The 2025 Nimdzi 100
sources.what_it_supports: Nimdzi's broad language-services definition includes translation, localization, interpretation, machine translation, data/AI services and generative-AI creation/editing, warning against direct NAICS mapping.
